Building Pyramids

Matt Polnik's blog

☰ Home About RSS

Egyptian pyramid construction techniques were described in detail in the Wikipedia article. This blog is neither about construction nor architecture.

I am a software engineer and a postgraduate research student at the University of Strathclyde in Glasgow. I created this site primarly to develop my writing skills by sharing solutions to technical challenges solved by me at work. Hopefully reading this blog will help you to learn someting new or at least save time if you are about to face similar problems.

You can meet me in the Intelligent Computational Engineering Laboratory at the 8th floor of the James Weir Building, where I work on the Robust Data-Driven Combinatorial Optimization project. Before moving to Scotland I used to work in industry. For more information on my previous projects and technical expertise please visit my LinkedIn profile.
